content.problemStatement:
Akhil has an array consisting of **N** positive integers. As we know that there are **(N*(N+1))/2** non-empty contiguous subarrays in an array of size **N**. Akhil found that exactly **E** of the contiguous subarrays had even sum and remaining **O = (N*(N+1))/2 - E** have odd sum.


Next day, he lost the original array. But he still remembered the **E** and **O** values correctly. Now, he wants to recreate any such array which has exactly **E** even sum continuous subarrays and **O** odd ones. If there are many such possible arrays, printing any one of them will make him happy.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows: First line of each test case contains an integer N denoting the length of lost array. Second line of each test case contains two space separated integers E and O, denoting number of contiguous subarrays with even and odd sums, respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all the integers of an array separated by a space. Each integer should lie between 0 and 100 (both inclusive). If there is no such possible array, print -1 in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there are 6 subarrays: {2}, {1}, {2}, {2,1}, {1,2}, {2,1,2}. Their sums are {2, 1, 2, 3, 3, 5} respectively. As 2 of them are even and 4 of them are odd, this is one possible answer which satisfies Akhil's requirements.
For this test case, the array [1,1,1,1] has 4 even subarrays and 6 odd subarrays.
For this test case, the array [2,2,2,2,1] has 10 even subarrays and 5 odd subarrays.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy approach. We start by initializing an empty array. Then, we try to add elements to the array such that the number of contiguous subarrays with even sum is maximized. We can achieve this by adding even numbers to the array. If the number of contiguous subarrays with even sum is already equal to E, we can add odd numbers to the array to maximize the number of contiguous subarrays with odd sum.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the length of the lost array. The time complexity is linear because we are iterating over the array once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the length of the lost array. The space complexity is linear because we are storing the array of length N.
